channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Real-World ICS Security Tales From the Trenches
item.description: SecurityWeek spoke with several ICS security experts and companies about their most memorable experiences in the field. The post Real-World ICS Security Tales From the Trenches appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Virtual Event Today: Threat Detection & Incident Response Summit
item.description: Don't miss this virtual event as we explore how to cut through alert fatigue, leverage AI and unified platforms to accelerate investigations, and apply actionable threat intelligence. The post Virtual Event Today: Threat Detection & Incident Response Summit appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Energy Bridge Technology Raises New Round Led by Monolith, CASVC Follows
item.description: Energy Bridge Technology (Shanghai) has completed a new funding round led by Monolith Capital, with CASVC participating. The company specializes in standardized, prefabricated superconducting power modules for AI data center infrastructure.

item.title: Tianji Intelligence Ships 10,000+ Force-Controlled Humanoid Arms in Q1, Unveils Four New Products
item.description: Guangdong Tianji Intelligence System has secured over 10,000 orders for force-controlled humanoid robot arms in Q1 2026, with a production line success rate exceeding 99.5%. The company has launched four new force-controlled humanoid arm products.

item.title: Chamsen Debuts at CIBF 2026 with All-Pole Tech for High-Performance Battery Track
item.description: Chamsen, a lithium battery startup founded in 2024 in Changzhou, is debuting at CIBF 2026. The company is targeting the structural supply-demand mismatch in all-pole lithium batteries for drones, humanoid robots, and data center BBU applications.

item.title: Lenovo Unveils Tianxi AI 4.0 Ecosystem: Palm-Sized AI Host, 95% Token Cost Reduction, First Large Foldable Phone
item.description: Lenovo Group has released the Tianxi AI 4.0 intelligent agent system, along with a full ecosystem of devices including a palm-sized AI host, PCs, smartphones, and tablets. Tianxi Claw features bionic memory, personal knowledge base, and AI simultaneous interpretation.

item.title: T-Head Unveils Zhengwu M890 AI Chip: 3x Performance Leap
item.description: Alibaba's chip design subsidiary T-Head has launched the Zhengwu M890, a next-generation training-and-inference AI chip. Official data shows overall performance improvements of up to 3x compared to the previous generation.

item.title: GitHub Confirms Hack Impacting 3,800 Internal Repositories
item.description: The TeamPCP hacking group accessed the repositories after a GitHub employee installed a poisoned VS Code extension. The post GitHub Confirms Hack Impacting 3,800 Internal Repositories appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: May 20, 08:49 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 20, 08:31 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.May 20, 08:14 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Microsoft Releases Mitigation for YellowKey BitLocker Bypass CVE-2026-45585 Exploit
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released a mitigation for a BitLocker bypass vulnerability named YellowKey following its public disclosure last week. The zero-day flaw, now tracked as CVE-2026-45585, carries a CVSS score of 6.8. It has been described as a BitLocker security feature bypass. "Microsoft is aware...

item.title: Grafana GitHub Breach Exposes Source Code via TanStack npm Attack
item.description: Grafana Labs, on May 19, 2026, said an investigation into its recent breach found no evidence of customer production systems or operations being compromised. It said the scope of the incident is limited to the Grafana Labs GitHub environment, which includes public and private source code along with...

item.title: GitHub Investigating TeamPCP Claimed Breach of ~4,000 Internal Repositories
item.description: GitHub on Tuesday said it's investigating unauthorized access to its internal repositories after the notorious threat actor known as TeamPCP listed the platform's source code and internal organizations for sale on a cybercrime forum. "While we currently have no evidence of impact to customer...

item.title: Position: Let's Develop Data Probes to Fundamentally Understand How Data Affects LLM Performance
item.description: arXiv:2605.18801v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Data is fundamental to large language models (LLMs). However, understanding of what makes certain data useful for different stages of an LLM workflow, including training, tuning, alignment, in-context learning, etc., and why, remains an open question....

item.title: Operationalizing Document AI: A Microservice Architecture for OCR and LLM Pipelines in Production
item.description: arXiv:2605.18818v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Academic research tends to focus on new models for document understanding creating a wide gap in the literature between model definition and running models at production scale. To close that gap, we present a microservice architecture that...

item.title: Evaluating the Utility of Personal Health Records in Personalized Health AI
item.description: arXiv:2605.18937v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Patient-managed Personal Health Records (PHRs) promises to empower patients to better understand their health; but information in the record is complex, potentially hindering insights. In this study, we assess the potential of large language models...

item.title: Learn-by-Wire Training Control Governance: Bounded Autonomous Training Under Stress for Stability and Efficiency
item.description: arXiv:2605.19008v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern language-model training is increasingly exposed to instability, degraded runs, and wasted compute, especially under aggressive learning-rate, scale, and runtime-stress conditions. This paper introduces Learn-by-Wire Guard (LBW-Guard), a bounded...

item.title: AgentNLQ: A General-Purpose Agent for Natural Language to SQL
item.description: arXiv:2605.19010v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Natural language to SQL (NL2SQL) conversion is an important problem for researchers and enterprises due to the ubiquitous importance of relational databases in broad-ranging practical problems. Despite the rapid advancements in the capabilities of...

item.title: Dimensional Balance Improves Large Scale Spatiotemporal Prediction Performance
item.description: arXiv:2605.18793v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate spatiotemporal pattern analysis is critical in fields such as urban traffic, meteorology, and public health monitoring. However, existing methods face performance bottlenecks, typically yielding only incremental gains and often exhibiting...

item.title: Robust Basis Spline Decoupling for the Compression of Transformer Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.18794v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decoupling is a powerful modeling paradigm for representing multivariate functions as compositions of linear transformations and univariate nonlinear functions. A single-layer decoupling can be viewed as a fully connected neural network with a single...

item.title: HELLoRA: Hot Experts Layer-Level Low-Rank Adaptation for Mixture-of-Experts Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.18795v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) dominates parameter-efficient fine-tuning of large language models, yet most variants target dense architectures. Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models scale parameters at near-constant per-token compute, and their sparse...

item.title: UCCI: Calibrated Uncertainty for Cost-Optimal LLM Cascade Routing
item.description: arXiv:2605.18796v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM cascades and model routing promise lower inference cost by sending easy queries to a small model and escalating hard ones to a large model, but most deployed routers use uncalibrated confidence scores and require per-workload threshold tuning. We...

item.title: Simply Stabilizing the Loop via Fully Looped Transformer
item.description: arXiv:2605.18797v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scaling model performance typically requires increasing model size. Looped Transformer offers a compelling alternative by iteratively reusing the same Transformer blocks, trading additional computation for improved performance without increasing...

item.title: The Annotation Scarcity Paradox in Low-Resource NLP Evaluation: A Decade of Acceleration and Emerging Constraints
item.description: arXiv:2605.19066v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Over the past decade, low-resource natural language processing (NLP) has experienced explosive growth, propelled by cross-lingual transfer, massively multilingual models, and the rapid proliferation of benchmarks. Yet this apparent progress masks a...

item.title: Benchmarking Commercial ASR Systems on Code-Switching Speech: Arabic, Persian, and German
item.description: arXiv:2605.19069v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Code-switching -- the natural alternation between two languages within a single utterance -- represents one of the most challenging and under-studied conditions for automatic speech recognition (ASR). Existing commercial ASR benchmarks predominantly...

item.title: ReacTOD: Bounded Neuro-Symbolic Agentic NLU for Zero-Shot Dialogue State Tracking
item.description: arXiv:2605.19077v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Task-oriented dialogue systems -- handling transactions, reservations, and service requests -- require predictable behavior, yet the moderately-sized LLMs needed for practical latency are prone to hallucination and format errors that cascade into...

item.title: Agent Meltdowns: The Road to Hell Is Paved with Helpful Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.19149v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agents operating with computer and Web use inevitably encounter errors: inaccessible webpages, missing files, local and remote misconfigurations, etc. These errors do not thwart agents based on state-of-the-art models. They helpfully continue to look...

item.title: Prompting language influences diagnostic reasoning and accuracy of large language models
item.description: arXiv:2605.19173v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly explored for clinical decision support, yet most evaluations are conducted in English, leaving their reliability in other languages uncertain. Here we evaluate the impact of prompting language on...

item.title: [AINews] Google I/O 2026: Gemini 3.5 Flash, Omni (NanoBanana for Video), Spark (background agents), and Antigravity 2.0
item.description: Google has been busy!

item.title: API users may see increased error rates for GPT-5.4 and GPT-5.5
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Chat Completions (Operational)

item.title: Verizon DBIR 2026: Vulnerability Exploitation Overtakes Credential Theft as Top Breach Vector
item.description: Verizon’s 2026 DBIR finds vulnerability exploitation has overtaken credential abuse as the leading breach vector, as AI accelerates attacks, patching delays worsen, and ransomware and third-party compromises continue to surge. The post Verizon DBIR 2026: Vulnerability Exploitation Overtakes...

item.title: Cloudflare Access AI control - MCP server creation impacted
item.description: May 19, 23:37 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 19, 22:09 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to investigate this issue.May 19, 21:59 UTCInvestigating - Customers may experience issue adding new MCP Servers or authenticating existing ones through the Zero Trust Dash

item.title: Roundtables: Inside the Musk v. Altman Trial
item.description: Listen to the session or watch below Elon Musk lost his suit against OpenAI, in which he alleged CEO Sam Altman and President Greg Brockman had deceived him over the company’s non-profit status. Watch as AI reporter and attorney Michelle Kim, who covered the trial for MIT Technology Review, joins...

item.title: Intermittent Cloudflare Dashboard SSO login failures with Okta
item.description: May 19, 20:10 UTCResolved - Cloudflare engineering has ruled out any service degrading within Cloudflare systems. Customers are advised to review their Okta authentication logs for potential errors. A status update will be provided if Cloudflare Engineering determines the specific Okta issue...

item.title: OlmoEarth v1.1: A more efficient family of models
Cloudflare Dashboard and Cloudflare API service issues
item.description: May 19, 16:48 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 19, 15:54 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.May 19, 15:36 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare is investigating issues with Cloudflare Dashboard and related APIs. These issues do not affect the serving...

item.title: Trapdoor Android Ad Fraud Scheme Hit 659 Million Daily Bid Requests Using 455 Apps
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new ad fraud and malvertising operation dubbed Trapdoor targeting Android device users. The activity, per HUMAN's Satori Threat Intelligence and Research Team, encompassed 455 malicious Android apps and 183 threat actor-owned command-and-control...

item.title: Drupal to Patch Highly Critical Vulnerability at Risk of Quick Exploitation
item.description: Drupal says attackers may develop an exploit for the vulnerability within hours or days. The post Drupal to Patch Highly Critical Vulnerability at Risk of Quick Exploitation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Digital Experience in Cloudflare Zero Trust Latency
item.description: May 19, 15:25 UTCInvestigating - Actions related to Digital Experience are delayed, Cloudflare is aware and investigating.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: May 19, 15:21 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to monitor for any further issues.May 19, 15:19 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.May 19, 14:03 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: DirtyDecrypt PoC Released for Linux Kernel CVE-2026-31635 LPE Vulnerability
item.description: Proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code has now been released for a recently patched security flaw in the Linux kernel that could allow for local privilege escalation (LPE). Dubbed DirtyDecrypt (aka DirtyCBC), the vulnerability was discovered and reported by the Zellic and V12 security team on May 9,...

item.title: Understanding the modern cybercrime landscape
item.description: Throughout 2025, HPE observed significant changes in how cybercriminals operate. Analyzing real-world threats, our HPE Threat Labs highlighted an industrialization of the cyber criminals’ methods in its new In the Wild Report, enabling greater scale, speed and structure in their campaigns. They...

item.title: The Download: Musk v. Altman, smart glasses for warfare, and Google I/O
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Here’s why Elon Musk lost his suit against OpenAI Elon Musk has lost his lawsuit against OpenAI, which centered on whether the company breached its...

item.title: Colossal Biosciences is growing chickens in a 3D-printed artificial eggshell
item.description: The baby chicks were shifting and starting to pip—or trying to hatch. But not from an egg.  Instead, these chickens were growing inside transparent 3D-printed plastic cups at the Dallas headquarters of Colossal Biosciences. The biotech company today claimed it has developed a “fully artificial...

item.title: Workers AI availability issues
item.description: May 19, 11:09 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.May 19, 10:56 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating availability issues with several workers AI models.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: May 19, 10:08 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 19, 08:41 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: [AINews] How to land a job at a frontier lab (on Pretraining)
item.description: a quiet day before google i/o lets us amplify a notable blogpost

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: May 19, 07:00 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude.ai and Claude Code login
item.description: May 19, 04:51 UTCResolved - This issue has been resolved.May 19, 04:46 UTCMonitoring - From 04:34–04:42 UTC, we saw elevated errors and latency on requests within the Claude.ai applications. Additionally, users may have experienced errors when attempting to login, including authenticating Claude...

item.title: Here’s why Elon Musk lost his suit against OpenAI
item.description: On Monday, the jury in Musk v. Altman dealt Elon Musk a major blow—reaching a unanimous advisory verdict that he had sued OpenAI too late and, as a result, his claims are barred by the applicable statutes of limitations. US District Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers immediately accepted it.  Musk...

item.title: Introducing the Ettin Reranker Family
